title: What Happens Next
excerpt:
Predicting the future is hazardous, but trends in motion tend to continue. Here's what I expect over the next decade.

AI will transform every industry, but not overnight. The hype cycle has compressed expectations into unrealistic timelines. Enterprise adoption will be slower than enthusiasts predict but faster than skeptics assume. The transformation will create enormous value, but it will take years, not months.

Vertical AI will outperform horizontal AI. Specialized applications with domain expertise and proprietary data will generate better outcomes than general-purpose tools. The foundation model layer will commoditize; the application layer will differentiate.
takeaways: The great companies of the next decade are being built now.
Economic downturns clear out weak competitors and force discipline on survivors.
Founders building during difficult times develop skills and cultures that serve them well.
The next generation of category-defining companies is emerging from this period.
The best opportunities emerge when others are retreating.
